event: slightly painful with palpation in the right iliac fossa
abdominal radiography revealed paraumbilical hydroaerial levels
suspicion of septic shock of intra-abdominal origin
empirical intravenous antibiotic therapy with third-generation cephalosporins
agglutinated intestinal loops in the right iliac fossa
purulent-appearing fluid collected for bacterial culture and sensitivity
jejunum and ileum overdistended and occlusive appearance
lavage and drainage of the peritoneal cavity
neglected peritonitis in the occlusive phase
acute granulocytic inflammatory infiltrate in greater omentum
general condition began to improve slowly
